title: Special Segments in a Circle

summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

A tangent and a secant are drawn from the same external point. If the tangent = 6 and the whole secant = 9, what is the external segment of the secant?

Chord-Chord Product If two chords intersect inside a circle, then the products of their segments are equal: AE × EB = CE × ED Secant-Secant Product If two secants are drawn from an external point, then: (whole₁)(external₁) = (whole₂)(external₂) Secant-Tangent Product If a secant and a tangent are drawn from the same external point, then: (tangen...

Two chords intersect inside a circle: AE = 3, EB = 8, CE = 4. What is ED?

Key circle theorems: a central angle equals its intercepted arc; an inscribed angle = ½ its intercepted arc; two chords intersecting inside form angles = ½(sum of arcs). The answer is 6. The other options — '5', '12', and '8' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

From an external point, a tangent has length 8 and a secant has an external segment of 4. What is the whole secant length?
